rulings.Case Title: YES IN MY BACK YARD, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 26, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER YES IN MY BACK YARD, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT, SONJA TRAUSS Motion And Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is continued to November 21, 2023 for the petitioner to file a supplemental brief addressing the procedural propriety of September 29, 2023 filing made 8 days after the previously set hearing date and without leave of Court. The Court notes that a response to this additional filing was filed on October 24, 2023, only 2 days before the hearing date. Additionally, all parties must file supplemental briefs (not to exceed 3 pages) addressing the substantive and procedural changes to the posture of this Petition in light of the Declaration of Richard Hannum filed on October 25, 2023, 1 day before the hearing. The supplemental briefs must be filed and served no later than November 1, 2023. Neither party is authorized to make any filings in support of or in opposition to the Petition for Writ of Mandate other than the filings described herein prior to the hearing on November 21, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1316-1330 JESSIE STREET, LLC VS. NATALIE TALKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Impose Sanction For Abuse Of Discovery, Including Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 26, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT NATALIE TALKER, SHAWN MCCALL Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Impose Sanction For Abuse Of Discovery, Including Terminating Sanctions Hearing Required. Plaintiff's counsel, Plaintiff's representative (Marcia Abad or any other member of Plaintiff with authority to verify discovery responses) and defendants to appear in person to engage in good faith meet and confer efforts regarding all outstanding discovery propounded by the Defendant. Parties and their counsel must appear at 8:00 a.m. Parties will report their progress at 9:30 a.m. If the Court determines that additional meet and confer efforts are required, parties will engage in further meet and confer efforts and report back at 11:45 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. on October 26, 2023; 11:45 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. on October 27, 2023. =(501/CFH)
